channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Palestinian death toll in Gaza tops 68,000, as Israel identifies the remains of one more hostage
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel said the remains of another hostage that Hamas handed over the day before were identified as Eliyahu Margalit, as the Palestinian death toll surpassed 68,000 people amid searching beneath the rubble.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: British military says ship ablaze after being struck off the coast of Yemen in the Gulf of Aden
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — A ship caught fire Saturday in the Gulf of Aden off Yemen after being struck by a projectile, the British military said, with one report suggesting its crew was preparing to abandon the vessel.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Jury awards $19M to bystanders wounded by Denver cop in 2022 shooting
item.description: A jury awarded $19.7 million to six bystanders who were shot and wounded by a Denver police officer who opened fire at an armed man in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: David Attenborough, 99, breaks record as oldest Daytime Emmy winner. 'General Hospital' wins big
item.description: PASADENA, Calif. (AP) — Paul Telfer of “Days of Our Lives” and Nancy Lee Grahn of “General Hospital” won lead acting honors at the Daytime Emmys on Friday, with ABC's “General Hospital” claiming six trophies in the major categories, including drama series.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dozens of costumed canines get their chance to trick-or-treating at Howloween event in Michigan
item.description: Dogs dressed up as everything from Elvis Presley to Scooby Doo’s Mystery Machine went trick-or-treating in Michigan.
item.dc:creator: Mike Householder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Legally blind tattoo artist shares journey
item.description: "At first, it was kind of like something that I felt like I had to warn people about,"Morrone said. Now, it's just really become part of my brand and just part of who I am, and I've embraced it.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Cloutier, NewsNation

item.title: Mother alleges son was sexually assaulted on Sallisaw school grounds
item.description: "I dropped my son off Tuesday morning thinking, you know, he's going to be safe," Beth said. "His whole world is just changed in a matter of 48 hours.
item.dc:creator: Gabriella Phelan, NewsNation

item.title: The other Alaska airlift: Volunteers save dogs from a flooded Alaska village, 1 tiny plane at a time
item.description: A nonprofit in rural Alaska is working to rescue dogs from Kipnuk, a village hit hard by Typhoon Halong.
item.dc:creator: Cedar Attanasio, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 78-year-old man missing in Oregon may have fallen in hole; police ask for help
item.description: In a phone call with his family, Thomas Weightman said he had fallen into a hole and did not know where he was.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, KRON4

item.title: Tensions rise ahead of 'No Kings' protests: What to know
item.description: Tensions have risen between critics and allies of the Trump administration as the country approaches the second "No Kings Day" of 2025.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, WFLA, NBC

item.title: With no takers yet, White House meets with colleges still weighing an agreement with Trump
item.description: Five remaining universities have been weighing an offer from President Donald Trump to sign a “compact” for higher education.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Father pleads guilty to murder of missing 7-month-old son in Southern California
item.description: RIVERSIDE, Calif. (AP) — The father of a 7-month-old boy missing in Southern California pleaded guilty to second-degree murder of his son after a monthslong investigation that has failed to locate the child's remains.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Disapproval of Trump rises in new Emerson College Polling survey: Political Power Ranker
item.description: President Donald Trump's disapproval rating continued to rise this month in the latest Emerson College Polling survey.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Meta adding AI chatbot safety features for teens
item.description: Meta, the parent company of Instagram and Facebook, plans to roll out new safety features for its artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: Evacuees detail harrowing scenes of flooding in coastal Alaska villages as airlift continues
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — The house rocked as if an earthquake had struck, and suddenly it was floating. Water seeped in through the front door, and waves smacked the big glass window.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Many swings, few knockout hits as Cuomo and Mamdani trade jabs in heated first NYC mayoral debate
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo entered Thursday evening’s first New York mayoral debate trying to blunt Democratic front-runner Zohran Mamdani's momentum.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Questions remain after women pleads guilty to corpse tampering in Austin man's death
item.description: A woman has pleaded guilty to corpse tampering in the death of Cristian Rangel, but police say the investigation into Rangel's death remains open and active.
item.dc:creator: Brianna Hollis, NewsNation

item.title: Dressers sold on Amazon recalled due to tip-over hazard
item.description: 76,500 dressers sold on Amazon are being recalled due to the risk of injury or death from a tip-over hazard.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, KRON4

item.title: Susan Stamberg, first woman to host a national news program, dies at age 87
item.description: Susan Stamberg, a “founding mother” of National Public Radio and the first female broadcaster to host a national news program, has died. She was 87. Stamberg died Thursday, NPR reported. It did not provide a cause of death.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: PA system takeover at Pennsylvania airport is 'wake-up call,' security expert says
item.description: An incident where an unauthorized user gained access to Harrisburg International Airport's public address system could have been much worse, a security expert said.
item.dc:creator: Harry Murphy, WGN 9

item.title: Mahmoud Khalil can freely travel around US as he fights his deportation case, judge rules
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge has lifted travel restrictions for Mahmoud Khalil, allowing the Palestinian activist to speak at rallies and other events across the U.S. as he fights his deportation case brought by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former Trump adviser John Bolton indicted over mishandling classified documents
item.description: A federal grand jury indicted former national security adviser John Bolton on charges of transmitting and retaining national defense information, bringing another criminal case against a critic of President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Energy Department offers $1.6 billion loan guarantee to upgrade transmission lines across Midwest
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Department of Energy said Thursday it has finalized a $1.6 billion loan guarantee to a subsidiary of one of the nation's largest power companies to upgrade nearly 5,000 miles of transmission lines across five states, mostly in the Midwest, for largely fossil fuel-run energy.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Microsoft: Russia, China increasingly using AI to escalate cyberattacks on the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Russia, China, Iran and North Korea have sharply increased their use of artificial intelligence to deceive people online and mount cyberattacks against the United States, according to new research from Microsoft.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: McConnell 'fine' after falling in Senate office building, spokesperson says
item.description: Sen. Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) fell on Thursday in front of left-wing protesters while walking through a Senate office building.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KRON4

item.title: MI5 chief says China is a security threat to UK as officials trade blame over spy case collapse
item.description: LONDON (AP) — China poses a daily threat to Britain’s security, the head of the country's domestic intelligence agency said Thursday, remarks that step up pressure on authorities to explain why the prosecution of two men charged with spying for Beijing collapsed just before they were due to stand tr
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Faulty engineering led to implosion of Titan submersible headed to Titanic wreckage, NTSB finds
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — Faulty engineering led to the implosion of an experimental submersible that killed five people on the way to the wreck of the Titanic, the National Transportation Safety Board concluded in a report Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump shutdown layoffs
item.description: A federal judge on Wednesday blocked President Trump’s efforts to lay off thousands of federal employees amid the government shutdown, for now. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dunkin' launches new Halloween-themed menu item
item.description: A new drink, the return of a fan-favorite, and a bucket? You can find them all at Dunkin' starting today.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: State Department revokes 6 visas over Charlie Kirk comments
item.description: The six individuals were identified as from Argentina, South Africa, Mexico, Brazil, Germany and Paraguay.
item.dc:creator: Meg Hilling, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DHS: US Coast Guard to get paid despite government shutdown
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on Tuesday announced that U.S. Coast Guard employees will get paid this week, despite the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, WGN 9

item.title: Rare October storm in Southern California prompts evacuations and causes freeway accidents
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A rare October storm pummeled Southern California with several inches of rain on Tuesday, swelling rivers, causing freeway accidents and prompting evacuations in wildfire-scarred neighborhoods where the threat of mudslides was high.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4
